raw_consumer_statement:
Issue : Credit card company refused to issue refund for unused travel service despite airline approval. 

What happened : I booked a flight from XXXX ( XXXX ) to XXXX to XXXX using my American Express card through the Amex Travel portal ( approx. {$1200.00} ), including Wi-Fi and baggage fees.

At the airport, I was denied boarding because my U.S. passport was expired and I didnt have a stamped XXXX XXXX for transit through XXXX XXXX even though I have XXXX citizenship and a valid XXXX approval ). This transit visa requirement was never disclosed on the Amex Travel booking portal. 

The airline ( XXXX XXXX ) informed me that I was eligible for a full refund and started processing it but then stopped when they saw the booking was through Amex Travel. They instructed me to contact Amex for the refund.

When I contacted Amex Travel, I was told I could only receive flight credit, not a refund. I reluctantly accepted flight credit under pressure, as I was told I would lose the entire amount if I was marked a no-show. Additionally, Amex did not issue Membership Rewards points for this transaction. 

Later, I had to rebook the exact same flight at full cost using the same Amex card because the return leg was canceled by Amex without my consent. I asked Amex to apply the original flight credit to this new booking ( same airline, same route ), but they refused. 

I ended up paying twice for the same ticket while losing the refund and points. 

What would be a fair resolution : A full refund to my American Express card for the original unused ticket OR Apply the flight credit to the amount I paid for the identical rebooked flight, and reinstate the missing Amex points. 

Company name : American Express
